raw_response:
<candidates>
<candidate_1>
<action>click('a47')</action>
<action_text>Click the list filter/funnel icon to open the filter builder</action_text>
<rationale>We need to create a filter where Problem statement contains a specific string; opening the filter builder is the standard first step.</rationale>
</candidate_1>
<candidate_2>
<action>click('a223', button='right')</action>
<action_text>Right-click the "Problem statement" column header to open its context menu</action_text>
<rationale>The header menu typically offers a Filter option pre-selecting this field, making it easy to set the operator to "contains" and enter the series value.</rationale>
</candidate_2>
<candidate_3>
<action>click('a75')</action>
<action_text>Click the list Search field</action_text>
<rationale>Focusing the list search allows a quick text filter; typing the series string can rapidly narrow results even before building a precise filter.</rationale>
</candidate_3>
<candidate_4>
<action>fill('a75', '#SERIES-2d813135-2')</action>
<action_text>Type "#SERIES-2d813135-2" into the list Search field</action_text>
<rationale>Quickly narrows the list to entries containing the series token, helping identify duplicates and verify the needed filter criteria.</rationale>
</candidate_4>
<candidate_5>
<action>click('79')</action>
<action_text>Open the All navigation panel</action_text>
<rationale>If we are not already in the "Assigned to me" module, opening the navigator lets us quickly switch to Problem > Assigned to me to satisfy step 1.</rationale>
</candidate_5>
</candidates>
